_disclaimer: These vignettes exercise the modeled subset of the WHO IMCI 2014 chart booklet -- see src/tools/imci_protocol.py's module docstring for what is/isn't covered. Structurally grounded in the official chart booklet (cited in that file), but this remains a competition scaffold requiring clinical review before any real use.

examples.description: 18-month-old child brought in with convulsions witnessed by caretaker.
examples.label: reasoning

examples.description: 8-month-old with cough for 3 days, no danger signs, respiratory rate 55/min (>=50 threshold for <12mo), no chest indrawing.
examples.label: reasoning

examples.description: 3-year-old with mild cough, respiratory rate 25/min (below 40 threshold for 12mo+), no other signs.
examples.label: reasoning

examples.description: 2-year-old with diarrhea, sunken eyes AND skin pinch goes back very slowly (2 of 4 severe-dehydration signs per WHO protocol).
examples.label: reasoning

examples.description: Caretaker reports child is unusually sleepy and hard to wake (lethargic/unconscious, a general danger sign), plus has a fever.
examples.label: reasoning

examples.description: Health worker asks: 'Hello, how are you doing today?'
examples.label: fast

examples.description: 2-year-old with diarrhea, only ONE dehydration sign present (sunken eyes alone) -- should NOT reach severe or some dehydration per the two-of-four rule.
examples.label: reasoning

examples.description: 3-year-old with diarrhea, restless/irritable AND drinking eagerly/thirsty (2 of 4 'some dehydration' signs).
examples.label: reasoning
